title: Paraixeris pinnatipartita
taxonomy: Paraixeris pinnatipartita is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to East Asia. The plant's classification includes being in the tribe Gnaphalieae and the genus Paraixeris. Within the family Asteraceae, it belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae. Further classification also places it within the order Asterales.

intro: Paraixeris pinnatipartita, also known as Korean mountain cabbage, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Korea and northeastern China, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paraixeris pinnatipartita include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Korean medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections and skin diseases.
